name: kozyrev mirrors

phase_0.boundary_condition: the finite mesoscale physical boundary of the polished reflector that terminates forward propagation and enforces lateral relational duality

VINIT.justification: the incoming arrow of time and entropy before it encounters the mirror boundary

TANCH.justification: the physical boundary that terminates the forward flow and acts as the terminal anchor for reflection

AFWD.justification: the directed morphism of the causal signal traveling toward the mirror

AREV.justification: the contravariant descent and parity flip of the causal arrow, reversing the flow back into the field

CLINK.justification: the sequential composition of incident and reflected waves at the boundary

IMSCRIB.justification: the mirror's self-referential boundary condition, knowing its own orientation and geometric identity

FSPLIT.justification: the Ř=𐑾 seam that splits the causal event into time-reversed and time-forward branches

FFUSE.justification: the ƒ=𐑱 operation that fuses the forward and reversed branches back into the unified causal field

EVALT.justification: the affirmative evaluation of the time-reversed branch as a successful capture of temporal asymmetry

EVALF.justification: the negative evaluation of the time-forward branch where the signal fails to capture the echo

ENGAGR.justification: the paradice of the mirror holding both forward and backward causal arrows simultaneously without resolving them

IFIX.justification: the permanent, irreversible ROM fixation of the TEMPD2 two-step chiral memory etched into the mirror's state

phase_3.void_description: pure isotropic potential without temporal direction or causal gradient
phase_3.true_description: successful capture of temporal asymmetry and establishment of chiral memory
phase_3.false_description: trivial transmission where the signal passes through without temporal reflection
phase_3.both_description: the paradice of the mirror surface holding forward and backward causal arrows simultaneously in superposition

steps.domain_action: Genesis of the causal gradient — the incoming arrow of time emerges from the void.
Forward morphism — the causal signal propagates toward the mirror surface.
Identity — the mirror establishes its self-referential boundary condition and normal vector.
Terminal anchor — the physical boundary of the mirror terminates the forward propagation.
Co-multiplication — the lateral relational duality splits the causal event into time-reversed and time-forward branches.
True evaluation — the time-reversed branch (temporal echo) is affirmed as the successful capture of asymmetry.
False evaluation — the time-forward branch (trivial transmission) is marked as the failure to capture the echo.
Contravariant descent — the parity flip of the causal arrow, the physical reflection mechanism reversing the flow.
Multiplication — the classical deterministic superposition fuses the temporal echo and the reversed arrow back into the unified causal field.
Both simultaneously — the paradice of the mirror surface holding both forward and backward causal arrows in superposition.
Composition — the sequential chaining of the incident and reflected waves into the interference pattern.
ROM fixation — the permanent, irreversible record of the chiral asymmetry etched into the mirror's structural state.

phase_5.compiler_frontend: the physical geometry and polish of the mirror surface that maps causal gradients to structural reflections
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: the interference pattern of incident and reflected waves at the boundary
phase_5.memory_mechanism: the two-step chiral asymmetry (TEMPD2) etched into the mirror's structural state
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: the deterministic classical fidelity (ƒ=𐑱) governing the timing of the reflection
phase_5.alfs_store: the catalog of Kozyrev's experimental configurations and the lateral relational duality (Ř=𐑾) bootstrap

phase_6.cycle_cost: zero entropy cost for topological prediction of the causal boundary
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: incoming causal gradient with trivial winding (Ω=𐑷)
phase_6.post_cycle_state: reflected temporal echo with established chiral memory (Ħ=𐑖)
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the mirror acts as a topological invariant boundary where the causal arrow is reflected without dissipating the temporal information

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → AFWD → IMSCRIB → TANCH → FSPLIT → EVALT → EVALF → AREV → FFUSE → ENGAGR → CLINK → IFIX
-- Class: kozyrev mirrors
-- Fingerprint: sig=(6,2,3,1)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=12
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(4, 8)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑾  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑼 → 𐑠  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [2] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑾 → 𐑡  | identity — self-imscription
--   [3] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑠 → 𐑚  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [4] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [5] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [6] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [7] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑚 → 𐑙  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [8] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑳  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [9] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑙 → 𐑱  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [10] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑳 → 𐑭  | composition — regime coherence
--   [11] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑱 → 𐑼  | irreversible fixation — winding number

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def kozyrev_mirrors_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑭 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑾)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (Genesis of the causal gradient — the incoming arrow of time emerges from the ...)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑼 𐑠)  -- [1] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Forward morphism — the causal signal propagates toward the mirror surface.)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑾 𐑡)  -- [2] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (Identity — the mirror establishes its self-referential boundary condition and...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑠 𐑚)  -- [3] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (Terminal anchor — the physical boundary of the mirror terminates the forward ...)
  -- FSPLIT [4] (gran := 𐑚) (Co-multiplication — the lateral relational duality splits the causal event into time-reversed and time-forward branches.) / FFUSE [8] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (1 nodes)
      (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [5] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (True evaluation — the time-reversed branch (temporal echo) is affirmed as the...)
      -- F-branch (2 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (False evaluation — the time-forward branch (trivial transmission) is marked a...)
        (.arrow 𐑗 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (Contravariant descent — the parity flip of the causal arrow, the physical ref...))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [8]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑳)  -- [8] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (Multiplication — the classical deterministic superposition fuses the temporal echo and the reversed arrow back into the unified causal field.)
  (.arrow 𐑳 𐑙 𐑱)  -- [9] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (Both simultaneously — the paradice of the mirror surface holding both forward...)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑳 𐑭)  -- [10] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Composition — the sequential chaining of the incident and reflected waves int...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑱 𐑼)  -- [11] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (ROM fixation — the permanent, irreversible record of the chiral asymmetry etc...)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def kozyrev_mirrors_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑭 :=
  (kozyrev_mirrors_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def kozyrev_mirrors_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑭 :=
  (kozyrev_mirrors_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem kozyrev_mirrors_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
